arts.ttl: Championship relegation run-in compared as Hull City and their rivals face moment of truth
arts.sum: Arguably Luton have the toughest run-in on paper with the Hatters facing Derby, Bristol City, Coventry and West Brom, while Cardiff and Plymouth's is equally tough. Just four games remain in the battle to stay in the Championship, and for the clubs at the wrong end of the league, the final two weeks of the season promises to be a nervous affair.

arts.ttl: Coventry City chief Frank Lampard makes Hull City weakness confession
arts.sum: Coventry City manager Frank Lampard admits he was aware of Hull City's issues at the start of the second half in games after watching his side become the latest to take advantage of the Tigers' slow start. For the third time in the four games since the international break, City conceded inside the opening 10 minutes of the second half, with Matt Grimes the latest to take full advantage with a scrambled volley which hit Charlie Hughes and found its way into the back of the net just a minute in.

arts.ttl: Joao Pedro relishing Hull City's huge Easter weekend - 'To die or to kill' - Hull Live
arts.sum: City's six-goal leading marksman remains positive that the season will have a happy outcome after what has largely been a forgettable campaign for the club who came so close to finishing in the play-offs 12 months ago. If you like football, it's an opportunity for us to show our character, to show that we deserve to be here, and I'm pretty sure that we will have a happy ending.

arts.ttl: Hull City Ladies chairman asks for club to be relegated ‘to try to punish players’
arts.sum: Players allege Daniel Johnson has mismanaged finances Club personnel report misconduct allegations to FA The chair of Hull City Ladies, Daniel Johnson, has submitted a request to the Football Association for the club to be relegated from the third tier, the Guardian has learned, amid allegations of mismanagement of the club’s finances being made against him by his own players. Multiple club sources have alleged that Johnson’s relegation submission is an attempt at retaliation after several squad members criticised him in public social media posts over the past 72 hours, with players expressing fears their team “might not exist in a few weeks”.
